segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: So Luke, for our next psychology assignment we have to do something on sleep and dreams.
segments.zh: 那么卢克，我们下一份心理学作业要写关于睡眠和梦的内容。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。我刚读到一篇文章，解释了为什么我们醒来后很快就会忘记大部分梦。我是说，我大多数梦也没那么有趣，但文章说如果我们什么都记得，可能会混淆实际发生的事和梦到的事。所以这算是一种保护。我以前没听过这个说法，一直以为只是我们记忆里装不下那么多东西。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: I've just read an article suggesting why we tend to forget most of our dreams soon after we wake up.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: I mean, most of my dreams aren't that interesting anyway, but what it said was that if we remembered everything, we might get mixed up about what actually happened and what we dreamed.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: So it's a sort of protection.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: I hadn't heard that idea before.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: I'd always assumed that it was just that we didn't have room in our memories for all that stuff.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Me too.
segments.zh: 我也是。你觉得梦可能预知未来这个想法怎么样？

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: What do you think about the idea that our dreams may predict the future?

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: It's a belief that you get all over the world.
segments.zh: 这是一种全世界都有的信仰。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Yeah, lots of people have a story of it happening to them, but the explanation I've read is that for each dream that comes true, we have thousands that don't, but we don't notice those, we don't even remember them.
segments.zh: 是啊，很多人都有过这种经历，但我读到的解释是，每一个成真的梦背后有成千上万个没成真的，但我们没注意到那些，甚至不记得它们。我们只记得那些现实中的某个东西，比如一个景象或一个动作，恰好触发了梦的记忆。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: We just remember the ones where something in the real world, like a view or an action, happens to trigger a dream memory.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对，所以其实只是巧合。我还读到一种叫分段睡眠的理论。就是说几百年前，人们习惯半夜起床聊聊天或吃点东西，然后再回去睡觉。我自己试过。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: So it's just a coincidence really.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Something else I read about is what they call segmented sleeping.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: That's a theory that hundreds of years ago, people used to get up in the middle of the night and have a chat or something to eat, then go back to bed.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: So I tried it myself.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Why?
segments.zh: 为什么？

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Well it's meant to make you more creative.
segments.zh: 据说能让人更有创造力。我也不知道为什么。但一周后我就放弃了，它跟我的生活方式不合拍。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: I don't know why.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: But I gave it up after a week.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: It just didn't fit in with my lifestyle.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: But most pre-school children have a short sleep in the day don't they?
segments.zh: 但大多数学龄前儿童白天都会小睡一会儿，对吧？上学期这里有些学生做了个实验，想看看孩子到多大就不需要午睡了。但他们没找到确切答案。他们花了很多时间设计最合适的研究方法，但结果似乎没显示出什么明显规律。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: There was an experiment some students did here last term to see at what age kids should stop having naps.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: But they didn't really find an answer.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: They spent a lot of time working out the most appropriate methodology, but the results didn't seem to show any obvious patterns.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。不管怎样，我们想想作业的事吧。上次我在最后阶段遇到了问题，就是得描述并论证我们觉得自己做得有多成功。我在行动计划上也费了点劲。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Anyway, let's think about our assignment.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Last time I had problems with the final stage, where we had to describe and justify how successful we thought we'd been.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: I struggled a bit with the action plan too.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: I was OK with the planning, but I got marked down for the self-assessment as well.
segments.zh: 我计划部分还行，但自我评估被扣分了。而且我在统计方面问题很大，那才是我丢分最多的地方。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: And I had big problems with the statistical stuff, that's where I really lost marks.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: So shall we plan what we have to do for this assignment?
segments.zh: 那我们规划一下这次作业要做什么吧？

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 行。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: First, we have to decide on our research question.
segments.zh: 首先，我们得确定研究问题。那么，“睡眠时长和做梦数量之间有关系吗？”这个怎么样？

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: So how about 'Is there a relationship between hours of sleep and number of dreams?

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好。然后我们需要考虑研究对象是谁。大约12个人？

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Then we need to think about who we'll do the study on.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: About 12 people?

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。那我们要用其他心理学学生吗？

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: And shall we use other psychology students?

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Let's use people from a different department.
segments.zh: 我们用其他系的人吧。历史系怎么样？

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: What about history?

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Yes, they might have interesting dreams!
segments.zh: 对，他们可能会做有趣的梦！或者文学系的学生？

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Or literature students?

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: I don't really know any.
segments.zh: 我其实不认识什么文学系的人。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: OK, forget that idea.
segments.zh: 好吧，那算了。那我们得考虑研究方法了。我们可以用观察法，但好像不太合适。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Then we have to think about our methodology.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: So we could use observation, but that doesn't seem appropriate.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: No. It needs to be self-reporting I think.
segments.zh: 不行。我觉得得用自我报告法。我们可以让他们在线回答问题。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: And we could ask them to answer questions online.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: But in this case, paper might be better as they'll be doing it straight after they wake up ... in fact while they're still half-asleep.
segments.zh: 但这样的话，纸质问卷可能更好，因为他们刚醒就要做……实际上可能还半睡半醒。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 对。我们还得查一下这类研究的伦理准则。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: And we'll have to check the ethical guidelines for this sort of research.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Mm, because our experiment involves humans, so there are special regulations.
segments.zh: 嗯，因为我们的实验涉及人类，所以有特殊规定。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Yes, I had a look at those for another assignment I did.
segments.zh: 是的，我之前做另一个作业时看过那些规定。有一整节是关于风险评估的，还有一节是关于确保他们不会承受任何不必要的压力。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: There's a whole section on risk assessment, and another section on making sure they aren't put under any unnecessary stress.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Let's hope they don't have any bad dreams!
segments.zh: 希望他们不会做噩梦！

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Then when we've collected all our data we have to analyse it and calculate the correlation between our two variables, that's time sleeping and number of dreams and then present our results visually in a graph.
segments.zh: 然后当我们收集完所有数据后，我们必须分析数据，计算两个变量之间的相关性，也就是睡眠时间和做梦次数，然后用图表直观地呈现结果。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。最后一步是思考我们的研究并对其进行评估。所以看起来相当简单。

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: And the final thing is to think about our research and evaluate it.

segments.speaker: LUKE
segments.en: So that seems quite straightforward.

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是的。那我们现在……

segments.speaker: SUSIE
segments.en: So now let's ...

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Dancing is something that humans do when they want to have a good time.
segments.zh: 跳舞是人类在想要享受快乐时会做的事情。这是对音乐的普遍反应，在所有文化中都能找到。但最近才发现，跳舞不仅让我们感觉良好，而且对健康也极其有益。跳舞，像其他形式的运动一样，会释放多巴胺等激素，让我们感到放松和快乐。它还能减轻压力或焦虑感。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It's a universal response to music, found in all cultures.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But what's only discovered recently is that dancing not only makes us feel good, it's also extremely good for our health.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Dancing, like other forms of exercise, releases hormones, such as dopamine, which make us feel relaxed and happy.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: And it also reduces feelings of stress or anxiety.

items.prompt: Luke read that one reason why we often forget dreams is that

pairs.p: tend to forget most of our dreams
pairs.note: 'often forget' 被替换为 'tend to forget most of'，意思相近但更具体。
'one reason why' 对应 'suggesting why'，表示解释原因。

paraphrase.explain: 题干问忘记梦境的原因，定位句开头提到 'suggesting why we tend to forget most of our dreams'，直接对应。

items.prompt: What do Luke and Susie agree about dreams predicting the future?

pairs.note: 题干中的'predicting the future'被替换为'comes true'，意思相同。

pairs.p: the explanation I've read is that
pairs.note: 题干中的'agree'对应原文中Luke提出的解释，表示他们认同这个观点。

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位句中的'explanation I've read'找到Luke和Susie共同认可的观点，即关于梦想成真的解释。

items.prompt: Susie says that a study on pre-school children having a short nap in the day

pairs.note: study 和 experiment 是同义替换，都表示研究。

pairs.q: pre-school children having a short nap in the day
pairs.p: pre-school children have a short sleep in the day
pairs.note: 题干和原文几乎逐字对应，只是时态不同。

paraphrase.explain: 题干中的 'study' 对应原文的 'experiment'，'pre-school children having a short nap in the day' 对应原文的 'pre-school children have a short sleep in the day'，由此定位到实验内容，但结果没有明显模式，所以选C。

items.prompt: In their last assignment, both students had problems with

pairs.note: 原文中'I'指代说话学生，题干'both students'包含该学生，所以'problems with'直接对应。
'last assignment'和'Last time'都指上一次作业，是同义替换。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句开头'let's think about our assignment. Last time I had problems with'直接对应题干'In their last assignment, both students had problems with'，注意'Last time'替换'last assignment'，'I had problems with'对应'had problems with'。

pairs.note: 题干说12个学生，原文说people，是概括性替换，但数量信息未直接对应，需注意上下文。
题干说25系，原文说不同系，是概括性替换，25系具体信息未出现。
题干问哪个系，原文直接给出history，是答案词。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'use people from a different department'，然后直接问'What about history?'，所以history就是答案。题干中的'Twelve students'和'25 department'在原文中没有直接出现，但通过'people'和'different department'进行了概括替换。

pairs.note: 'on 26'（在第26页）被替换为'in this case'（在这种情况下），表示同一情境。
答案'paper'在原文中直接出现，完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 通过'Answers'和'paper'的完全匹配，以及'on 26'被替换为'in this case'，可以定位到原文中'paper'作为答案。

items.prompt: Check ethical guidelines for working with 27

pairs.note: 题干和原文完全一致，直接定位
题干中的'working with 27'被替换为'this sort of research'，指代同一研究

paraphrase.explain: 通过定位'ethical guidelines'找到原文，再理解'this sort of research'指代的是题干中的'working with 27'，从而确定答案。

items.prompt: Ensure that risk is assessed and 28 is kept to a minimum

pairs.note: 题干是动词短语，原文是名词短语，意思相同。
题干说'保持最小化'，原文说'不承受不必要的'，表达同一概念。
题干用数字28指代答案，原文直接出现stress。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'risk assessment'和'aren't put under any unnecessary stress'，与题干'risk is assessed'和'kept to a minimum'对应，从而找到答案stress。

items.prompt: Calculate the correlation and make a 29

pairs.p: present our results visually in a
pairs.note: 原文用'present...visually in a'替换了题干中的'make a'，意思相同。
题干和原文中都是'graph'，完全一致。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到'present our results visually in a graph'，其中'graph'与题干相同，而'present...visually in a'对应'make a'，因此答案就是graph。

pairs.note: 题干中的'the research'对应原文中的'our research'，都是指同一项研究。
题干中的'30'是题号，原文中'final thing'表示最后一项，对应第30题。

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干中的'the research'和'30'定位到原文的'final thing'和'our research'，然后听到'evaluate'即为答案。
